thesis: ONGC screens cheaper than peers while analyst revisions are strongly positive; as a state oil major it pays a high, well-covered dividend — the core value_revision dividend holding.
falsification: Crude rolls over and analyst revisions turn negative, or a windfall-tax/subsidy hit cuts the dividend.

thesis: NMDC is cheap on cheapness z and analysts are upgrading; low-cost ore producer with a generous payout fits the dividend-yield mandate.
falsification: Steel demand/ore prices weaken, revisions fade, or captive-mine policy hurts realisations.

thesis: Coal India is a structural cash-cow with one of the highest yields on the exchange and still-constructive revisions; the anchor dividend name.
falsification: Revisions drop below neutral or an accelerated energy-transition/volume miss threatens the payout.

thesis: LIC trades cheap with strongly positive analyst revisions and pays a meaningful dividend; a large under-owned franchise where the value_revision brain is most aligned.
falsification: VNB margins/persistency disappoint and revisions turn down, or equity-market drawdown hits embedded value.

thesis: Muthoot is cheap with positive revisions and steady dividends; gold-loan AUM growth supports the upgrade cycle.
falsification: Gold-loan regulation tightens or gold prices fall sharply, reversing revisions.

thesis: Gujarat Gas screens cheap with strongly rising revisions as gas spreads improve; a regulated, cash-generative dividend payer.
falsification: APM gas allocation cuts or LNG spikes compress margins and revisions reverse.

thesis: Chennai Petroleum is deeply cheap with rising revisions on firm refining margins; a high-payout cyclical for the value sleeve.
falsification: Refining (GRM) margins collapse and revisions turn down, or a heavy capex cycle cuts the dividend.

thesis: Chambal is cheap with strongly positive revisions; stable subsidy-backed cash flows support a steady payout.
falsification: Subsidy-policy change or gas-cost spike cuts margins and revisions roll over.

thesis: Great Eastern Shipping is cheap with rising revisions and one of the few names here with positive price momentum; high cyclical dividend payout.
falsification: Tanker/dry-bulk freight rates fall and revisions reverse.

thesis: Karnataka Bank trades cheap with positive revisions as asset quality and ROA improve; a value financial with a dividend.
falsification: Credit costs rise or NIM compresses and revisions fade.

thesis: Satin Creditcare is cheap with rising revisions as the MFI cycle stabilises; a tactical small-cap value tilt.
falsification: MFI asset-quality stress re-emerges and revisions turn negative.

thesis: Equitas SFB carries strongly positive revisions as it transitions toward universal-bank economics; a value financial add.
falsification: Microfinance book deteriorates or transition costs spike, reversing revisions.

thesis: Grasim has strongly positive revisions across its cement/VSF/paints stack and pays a dividend; quality ballast for the value book.
falsification: Paints-foray losses widen or VSF/cement margins disappoint and revisions soften.

thesis: Asian Paints is now reasonably valued after a long de-rating with revisions turning supportive; a quality dividend compounder bought on weakness.
falsification: Competitive intensity (new entrants) keeps margins under pressure and revisions stay negative.

thesis: Coforge retains strong positive revisions versus a weakening IT cohort; keep as the preferred IT exposure but at a controlled size.
falsification: Deal momentum slows, revisions fade, or discretionary-tech spend stays weak.

thesis: Bajaj Auto pays well and carries reasonably firm revisions; premium two-wheeler/export franchise as auto ballast.
falsification: Export/EV-transition pressure cuts volumes and revisions turn down.

thesis: Power Grid offers a regulated-return, high-payout profile with neutral revisions; a low-beta dividend anchor.
falsification: Regulatory return cuts or a large unfunded capex cycle pressures the payout.

thesis: NTPC is cheap with a dependable dividend and a capacity-growth pipeline; defensive yield exposure.
falsification: Coal/PLF disruption or revisions turning clearly negative.

thesis: SBI is cheap on cheapness z with a reasonable dividend; benchmark-relevant bank exposure despite only neutral revisions.
falsification: Credit costs rise materially or revisions deteriorate further.

thesis: Axis is fairly valued with revisions modestly positive; private-bank exposure to balance the PSU tilt.
falsification: NIM compression or asset-quality slippage reverses revisions.

thesis: HDFC Bank is cheap post-merger with stabilising metrics; a low-tracking-error core financial even with neutral revisions.
falsification: Deposit-cost pressure persists and revisions slip further negative.

thesis: ITC pays one of the steadiest large-cap dividends and is fairly valued; a defensive yield ballast.
falsification: Cigarette taxation shock or revisions turning clearly negative.

thesis: BEL has a strong order book and pays a dividend with neutral-to-firm revisions; structural defense exposure.
falsification: Order inflow slows or margins compress and revisions turn down.

thesis: HAL has multi-year defense visibility and a dividend; modest weight given neutral revisions and rich valuation.
falsification: Execution delays or order slippage reverse the earnings trajectory.

thesis: Vedanta is cheap with positive price momentum and a very high dividend; I express the whole Vedanta view through the liquid parent rather than the illiquid demerger stubs.
falsification: Commodity prices fall, the demerger value-unlock stalls, or leverage at the promoter level forces a dividend cut.

thesis: Aptus has structural affordable-housing growth and a dividend; keep a modest weight pending a revision pickup.
falsification: Asset-quality stress in affordable housing or revisions turning negative.

thesis: Angel One is a high-payout broker but revisions have cooled with market-volume normalisation; reduce to a modest weight.
falsification: Cash-equity volumes and revisions re-accelerate, justifying a re-add.

thesis: MCX has a structural monopoly and positive price momentum with neutral revisions; keep for capital-markets breadth.
falsification: Volume/regulatory shock or revisions turning down.

thesis: CMS Info is cheap with a steady model but revisions have softened; hold a reduced weight.
falsification: Revisions stay negative or ATM/cash-management volumes decline.

thesis: CAMS is a capital-markets-plumbing duopoly with a dividend; modest weight pending a revision pickup.
falsification: MF-flow slowdown or pricing pressure reverses revisions.

thesis: Thyrocare keeps moderately positive revisions; keep a reduced tactical weight in diagnostics.
falsification: Pricing competition in diagnostics intensifies and revisions roll over.

thesis: Kewal Kiran is a debt-free, high-payout apparel name with still-acceptable revisions; modest weight.
falsification: Discretionary apparel demand weakens and revisions fall.

thesis: Infosys is cheap with a solid dividend; keep as the large-cap IT anchor at a reduced size given soft sector revisions.
falsification: Discretionary-tech demand stays weak and revisions deteriorate further.

thesis: TCS pays a strong dividend but sector revisions are soft; hold a reduced weight as quality IT ballast.
falsification: IT-services revisions keep falling and growth guidance is cut.

thesis: Tech Mahindra's turnaround is incomplete and revisions are soft; minimal weight only.
falsification: Margin-recovery program stalls and revisions stay negative.

thesis: Mphasis keeps only modest revisions amid soft IT spend; minimal tactical weight.
falsification: BFSI-tech demand recovers and revisions turn up.

thesis: Shriram Finance is fairly valued with a dividend but revisions have cooled; modest weight.
falsification: Vehicle-finance credit costs rise or revisions fall further.

thesis: HDFC Life is a structural protection-growth name but revisions are soft; modest weight pending an upgrade.
falsification: Margin/persistency disappoints and revisions stay negative.

thesis: Hero MotoCorp pays a high dividend but revisions are soft amid EV transition; reduced weight.
falsification: Rural two-wheeler demand stays weak and EV share keeps eroding the core.

thesis: GAIL pays a dividend but revisions have turned down; cut to a minimal weight.
falsification: Transmission-tariff hikes or petchem recovery turn revisions positive again.

thesis: Dabur is a steady dividend FMCG but revisions are soft on rural-demand weakness; minimal weight.
falsification: Rural recovery lifts volumes and revisions turn up.

thesis: HUL is a high-quality dividend payer but offers little value-revision edge here; minimal defensive weight.
falsification: Margin/volume disappointment with revisions turning negative.

thesis: ICICI Pru AMC is a capital-markets dividend play but revisions have cooled; minimal weight.
falsification: MF-flow momentum and revisions re-accelerate.

thesis: CESC is a cheap regulated utility with a dividend but only neutral revisions; reduced weight.
falsification: Distribution-loss reduction stalls or revisions fall.

thesis: Indus Towers is cheap with a dividend but revisions have softened on tenancy concerns; minimal weight.
falsification: Tenancy adds re-accelerate and revisions turn up.

thesis: Balrampur Chini has an ethanol tailwind and a dividend but revisions are soft; modest weight.
falsification: Sugar/ethanol policy turns adverse and revisions fall.
